project.name: SecureAuth Platform
project.description: User authentication and authorization system with JWT-based session management.

specifications.title: User Authentication Flow
specifications.description: Implement the complete user authentication flow from database schema through API endpoints, middleware, and integration testing.

epics.title: Authentication System
epics.description: Full authentication stack from database to integration tests, with clear dependency ordering between tickets.
epics.objective: Deliver a production-ready authentication system with proper dependency sequencing.

tickets.title: Create users table and auth database schema
tickets.description: Create the PostgreSQL migration for the users table including columns for email, password_hash, role, email_verified, and timestamps. This is the foundational ticket with no dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: users table created with id, email, password_hash, role, email_verified, created_at, updated_at
Unique constraint on email column
Migration rolls forward and back cleanly
UserRepository with create, findByEmail, findById methods

steps: Create Knex migration for users table
Add unique index on email
Create UserRepository class
Write repository unit tests

tickets.title: Implement auth service with password hashing and JWT
tickets.description: Build the AuthService class that handles user registration, login with bcrypt password verification, and JWT token generation/validation. Requires the users table and repository from ticket 1.
acceptanceCriteria: register() hashes password with bcrypt and creates user
login() verifies password and returns JWT access + refresh tokens
validateToken() verifies JWT signature and expiry
Passwords are never stored or logged in plaintext

steps: Create AuthService with register, login, validateToken methods
Implement bcrypt password hashing with configurable salt rounds
Implement JWT sign/verify with RS256 algorithm
Add refresh token rotation logic
Write unit tests with mocked UserRepository

tickets.title: Create authentication API endpoints
tickets.description: Implement POST /api/auth/register, POST /api/auth/login, and POST /api/auth/refresh Express route handlers that delegate to AuthService. Requires the auth service from ticket 2.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/register creates user and returns 201 with tokens
POST /api/auth/login returns 200 with tokens for valid credentials
POST /api/auth/login returns 401 for invalid credentials
POST /api/auth/refresh rotates refresh token
Request validation with Zod schemas on all endpoints

steps: Create auth router with register, login, refresh routes
Add Zod request validation schemas
Wire routes to AuthService methods
Add rate limiting on login endpoint
Write integration tests with Supertest

tickets.title: Implement authentication middleware
tickets.description: Create Express middleware that extracts JWT from Authorization header, validates it via AuthService, and attaches the authenticated user to the request context. Requires the auth service (ticket 2) for token validation and the API endpoints (ticket 3) to be defined so middleware can be applied to protected routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Middleware extracts Bearer token from Authorization header
Valid tokens attach user object to req.user
Invalid or expired tokens return 401
Missing Authorization header returns 401
Middleware is composable with role-based authorization

steps: Create authMiddleware function that validates JWT
Create requireRole middleware factory for role-based access
Extend Express Request type with user property
Apply middleware to protected routes
Write unit tests with mock tokens

tickets.title: End-to-end authentication integration tests
tickets.description: Write comprehensive integration tests covering the full authentication flow: registration, login, token refresh, authenticated requests via middleware, and error cases. Depends on all previous tickets being complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Full registration -> login -> access protected route flow passes
Token refresh flow works end-to-end
Expired token is rejected by middleware
Invalid credentials are rejected at login
Role-based access control is enforced
All tests run against test database with proper setup/teardown

steps: Set up test database with migrations
Write registration flow test
Write login flow test with token validation
Write refresh token rotation test
Write protected route access test
Write role-based authorization test
Write error case tests (invalid credentials, expired tokens, missing headers)
Add CI pipeline configuration for integration tests
